title: Runbook / Checklist (SOP)
description: Data contract for the runbook-checklist format: an operational, action-oriented standard-operating-procedure rendered as numbered sections of checkable steps with optional commands, expected-results, rollback notes and critical callouts, plus a sticky progress rail and an always-visible rollback plan. The #report-data instance in runbook_checklist_sample.html is the canonical example. Default theme: cyan.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this runbook. When set, it is the localStorage key for saved checklist state (so progress survives meta.title / project edits); otherwise a key is derived from meta.project + meta.title.

meta.description: Shared report identity block (see _DATA_DRIVEN.md). Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}}/{{PROJECT}}/{{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

org.description: Organisation wordmark shown in header + footer. Placeholder: {{ORG}}.

project.description: Project / system this procedure applies to. Placeholder: {{PROJECT}}.

reportType.description: Artefact type label, e.g. 'Runbook' or 'Operational SOP'.

title.description: Procedure title — the operational objective, stated as a goal.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line context shown under the title (the trigger / scope).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; set on <html data-theme>. Live-switchable via .rc-theme. Default cyan for this format.

severity.description: Incident / change severity. Drives the header severity pill colour: sev1/sev2 -> risk (bordeaux), sev3 -> hold (amber), info -> ok.

estDuration.description: Human-readable estimated duration for the whole procedure, shown in the header.

owners.description: People / roles accountable for executing the runbook; rendered as mono owner-chips in the header.

prerequisites.description: The 'before you begin' gate: preconditions, access, and tools required before step 1. Each item is a plain string or an object with an optional note.

note.description: Optional clarifying note (e.g. where to get access).

sections.description: Ordered, numbered phases of the procedure. Each section is a collapsible group of checkable steps with a live done/total counter. Recommended 3-4 sections.

id.description: Stable slug used for the rail jump-link anchor and progress tracking.

steps.description: The checkable steps in this section, in execution order.

id.description: Stable unique step id (used as the key in the in-memory done-set and for the checkbox).

text.description: The step instruction — imperative, atomic, one action.
examples: Drain node web-07 from the load balancer and confirm zero in-flight requests.

command.description: Optional command(s) to run for this step; rendered in a dark mono block with a copy button. May contain newlines for multi-line commands.

expected.description: Optional expected-result line ('you should see ...'); rendered in an ok-toned line with a check-circle icon.

rollback.description: Optional 'if this fails, do ...' note for this step; rendered in a hold-toned inset.

critical.description: If true, marks the step as destructive / no-undo; rendered with a risk-toned critical callout. Pair with a rollback note where possible.

rollbackPlan.description: The overall rollback / abort procedure, shown in an always-visible card in the sticky rail so the operator can bail out at any point.

summary.description: One-line statement of when to invoke rollback.

meta.title: Mitigate duplicate-disbursement incident on the settlement path
meta.subtitle: SEV2 — duplicate payments clearing under partial-failure retry. Stabilise, stop the bleed, verify, close.
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

prerequisites: PagerDuty incident opened and bridge call active.

prerequisites.text: Production kubectl + lbctl access to the payments cluster.
prerequisites.note: Request via break-glass if you lack standing access.

prerequisites: Read access to the settlement warehouse for the dedup query.
Change ticket reference to hand off to the post-incident review.

steps.text: Confirm the duplicate-disbursement alert is firing and not a false positive.
steps.expected: At least one firing alert with non-zero `dup_count` in the last hour.

steps.text: Run the dedup query to count confirmed duplicate ledger pairs in the live window.
steps.command:
psql -h warehouse -c \
  "SELECT count(*) FROM dup_breaks WHERE created_at > now()-interval '6h';"
steps.expected: A concrete count of affected request ids; record it in the incident channel.

steps.text: Raise the client retry budget temporarily to shrink the post-commit gap window.
steps.command: kubectl -n payments set env deploy/settle-orchestrator RETRY_BUDGET_MS=15000
steps.rollback: Restore with RETRY_BUDGET_MS=5000 if latency SLO breaches.

steps.text: Enable the same-day duplicate reconciliation alert to cut detection lag to under one hour.
Place a unique index on (account_id, request_id) to block any further duplicate persist at the DB.
steps.command:
psql -h ledger-primary -c \
  "CREATE UNIQUE INDEX CONCURRENTLY ux_ledger_req ON ledger_entries(account_id, request_id);"
steps.expected: Index builds without error (no existing duplicates block it).
steps.rollback: If the build fails on existing duplicates, reverse those rows first (see rollback plan), then retry.

steps.text: Re-run the dedup query and confirm no new duplicate pairs since stabilisation.
steps.command:
psql -h warehouse -c \
  "SELECT count(*) FROM dup_breaks WHERE created_at > now()-interval '30m';"
steps.expected: Count is `0` for the post-mitigation window.

steps.text: Reverse the confirmed duplicate disbursements and notify reconciliation.
steps.rollback: Reversals are themselves logged; do not re-run the reversal job twice for the same id.

steps.text: Hand off to the post-incident review and link the change ticket; lower severity once stable for 30 min.

rollbackPlan.summary: If stabilisation makes things worse (latency SLO breach or failed index build), back out in this order.
steps: Drop the unique index: DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY ux_ledger_req.
Announce rollback on the bridge and keep the incident open at SEV2.
